dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, the situation has become far more dangerous than either of us anticipated.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Someone in {location} discovered what we've been doing with {quest_focus}. {target_npc} is being pressured to make a public statement condemning our actions, and a patrol has been organized to hunt down {quest_focus} and destroy it. Meanwhile, the {enemy_type} have sent their own search party {location_hint}, and if they find {quest_focus} before we can move it to safety, neither side will show restraint. I need you to gather {quest_item} to create a distraction and buy us time to {quest_action}.
dialogueChunks.outro: Both sides are closing in. If we don't act now, {quest_stakes} dies on the altar of someone else's fear.

playerResponses.text: I'll create the opening. Get ready to move.
Two hostile groups converging on one point? This is a mess. But I'll do it.
Both armies hunting the same thing? I'm not stepping into that crossfire.

dialogueChunks.intro: They came for {quest_focus} last night, {player_name}. We barely got away.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The mob that formed in {location} wasn't interested in explanations. They wanted blood, and {target_npc} tried to talk them down but got shouted into silence. We managed to relocate {quest_focus} to a hiding spot {location_hint}, but we can't stay there long. The {enemy_type} raids have intensified as if they can sense the disruption in our settlement. I need {quest_item} to sustain {quest_focus} and keep them hidden while I try to {quest_action} through back channels.
dialogueChunks.outro: {quest_threat} is closing in from every direction. We need supplies and we need them fast.

playerResponses.text: Tell me what's needed. I'll keep {quest_focus} safe.
Angry mobs and enemy raids at the same time. I hope this is worth it.
You're fighting the whole settlement and the enemy. I can't win that war.

dialogueChunks.intro: The {enemy_type} sent an emissary, {player_name}. And our people nearly killed it on sight.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The emissary carried a message: they want {quest_focus} returned, and they're willing to offer {quest_item} in exchange and stop raiding {location_hint}. But our guards intercepted the messenger and now {target_npc} is caught between people who want war and an enemy offering something that looks dangerously like peace. We need someone to reach the {enemy_type} camp {location_hint} and confirm the offer is genuine before {quest_threat} turns this into a massacre.
dialogueChunks.outro: If their offer is real, {quest_action} could end years of bloodshed. If it's not, at least we'll know the truth.

playerResponses.text: I'll go to their camp. If peace is possible, someone has to try.
Walking into an enemy camp alone. Wonderful. I'll need assurances when I get back.
Walking into the enemy's camp unarmed is suicide, not diplomacy.
